cells.controlling_rule: COPPA binds. The 2025 Rule amendments (16 CFR 312.10) are fully in force (published 22 Apr 2025; effective 23 June 2025; compliance deadline 22 Apr 2026 — passed). They require: (1) a mandatory WRITTEN data-retention policy stating collection purposes, business need, and a specific deletion timeframe, disclosed in the privacy notice; (2) retention 'only as long as is reasonably necessary' for the collection purposes; (3) an express prohibition on INDEFINITE retention. There is NO numerical cap — operators set their own 'reasonable and specific' deletion timeframes. Scope: under-13 data only (not 13–17). Retention values deferred to counsel per data-model.md §4.9 (the `person_retain` seam).

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA's actual-knowledge doctrine: account existence alone (Family Sharing / Google Family Link) is NOT a shield — but it is not a trigger either, in the unknown-knowledge state. Standard COPPA 'directed to children' / 'actual knowledge' analysis applies. Caveat (verified 2026-06-05): platform-provided age signals (Apple Declared Age Range API / Google Play Age Signals) can confer actual/constructive knowledge of a minor and so TRIGGER obligations, even though account existence does not satisfy consent verification; both vendors scope Family Sharing/Family Link consent to their own services — third-party apps must obtain their own.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA's actual-knowledge doctrine has been triggered. The '3b posture' (account existence as consent signal) is 'unclear, not no' — verified 2026-06-05: the FTC's 2025 Rule declined a broad rebuttable VPC presumption from device/account sharing (limited rebuttal only via the mixed-audience path), so Family Sharing / Family Link is not a sanctioned VPC method but not categorically barred. No-DOB/no-age-capture design maps to the actual-knowledge trigger (FTC expressly rejected a 'reason to know' standard), but actual knowledge folds in willful disregard — a posture, not a safe harbor. Wildec (2019 warning letter) and US v. TikTok (MTD largely denied 6 Nov 2025, no final judgment) show knowledge from signals operators DID collect — they prove the enforcement floor, not the no-collection escape. The Feb 2026 FTC policy statement is permissive enforcement discretion for voluntary age-verifiers, imposing no new duty on non-collectors. Dispositive risk pivot: if content/marketing reads as child-directed / mixed-audience, the engineered-ignorance posture is materially weaker. Counsel must rule.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Arguable: VPC can be substituted by 'engineered ignorance' — but the shield evaporates the moment a child types 'I'm 10' in chat, and it never works if the service is classified child-directed/mixed-audience
cells.confidence_rationale: Verified + tightened 2026-06-05 adversarial pass: FTC 2025 Rule presumption-decline, Wildec/TikTok litigation status, and Feb 2026 policy statement all confirmed; the posture survives but is actor-classification-dependent.
